source_paper_title: Indefinite Linear-Quadratic Partially Observed Mean-Field Game
context:
Section 4.3 (Feedback representation), Remark 4.13(ii), page 14.

Noticing that Riccati equation (35) is an asymmetric indefinite equation with complex structure, its solvability is still an problem.
title: Solvability of the asymmetric indefinite Riccati equation \((35)\) arising in partially observed indefinite LQ mean-field games
background_latex:
Let \(T>0\). Let \(A,\bar A,D,\bar D\in L^{\infty}([0,T];\mathbb R^{n\times n})\), \(B,\bar B,F,\bar F\in L^{\infty}([0,T];\mathbb R^{n\times m})\), and \(Q\in L^{\infty}([0,T];\mathbb S^n)\), \(R\in L^{\infty}([0,T];\mathbb S^m)\), \(S\in L^{\infty}([0,T];\mathbb R^{n\times m})\), \(L_T\in\mathbb S^n\). Let \(\alpha_1,\alpha_3,\beta_1,\beta_2\in\mathbb R\) be constants.

For an unknown matrix-valued function \(\Sigma:[0,T]\to\mathbb R^{n\times n}\), define for each \(t\in[0,T]\)
- \(\bar B_t := B_t+\bar B_t\) (for compactness of notation in this problem statement),
- \(F^{\mathrm{mf}}_t := F_t+\bar F_t\),
- \(R^{\mathrm{e}}_t(\Sigma) := (1-\beta_1)R_t + F_t^{\top}\Sigma_t F^{\mathrm{mf}}_t\in\mathbb R^{m\times m}\),
- \(\Sigma^{\mathrm{e}}_t(\Sigma) := \Sigma_t \bar B_t + D_t^{\top}\Sigma_t F^{\mathrm{mf}}_t + (1-\beta_2)S_t\in\mathbb R^{n\times m}\),
- \(\bar\Sigma_t(\Sigma) := \Sigma_t \bar B_t + (D_t+\bar D_t)^{\top}\Sigma_t F_t + (1-\alpha_2)S_t\in\mathbb R^{n\times m}\), where \(\alpha_2\in\mathbb R\) is a given constant.

The Riccati-type terminal value problem considered in the paper is
\[\dot\Sigma_t + \Sigma_t(A_t+\bar A_t) + A_t^{\top}\Sigma_t + D_t^{\top}\Sigma_t(D_t+\bar D_t) - \Sigma^{\mathrm{e}}_t(\Sigma)\,\big(R^{\mathrm{e}}_t(\Sigma)\big)^{-1}\,\bar\Sigma_t(\Sigma)^{\top} + (1-\alpha_1)Q_t = 0,\qquad \Sigma_T = (1-\alpha_3)L_T.\]
This equation is generally asymmetric and may be indefinite.
problem_definition_latex:
Problem: Establish verifiable necessary and/or sufficient conditions on the coefficient data \(A,\bar A,D,\bar D,B,\bar B,F,\bar F,Q,R,S,L_T\) and constants \(\alpha_1,\alpha_2,\alpha_3,\beta_1,\beta_2\) under which the asymmetric indefinite Riccati terminal value problem
\[\dot\Sigma_t + \Sigma_t(A_t+\bar A_t) + A_t^{\top}\Sigma_t + D_t^{\top}\Sigma_t(D_t+\bar D_t) - \Sigma^{\mathrm{e}}_t(\Sigma)\,\big(R^{\mathrm{e}}_t(\Sigma)\big)^{-1}\,\bar\Sigma_t(\Sigma)^{\top} + (1-\alpha_1)Q_t = 0,\qquad \Sigma_T = (1-\alpha_3)L_T,\]
admits a (preferably unique) solution \(\Sigma\) on \([0,T]\) such that \(R^{\mathrm{e}}_t(\Sigma)\) is invertible for all \(t\in[0,T]\).
